title: 10 best herbal decoctions for coughing fits
intro_desc: Herbal decoctions for coughing fits are a natural and effective way to soothe and calm persistent coughs. These decoctions are made by steeping herbs in hot water, releasing their medicinal properties and relieving congestion and inflammation in the throat and chest. Examples of herbal decoctions that help with coughing fits include thyme, eucalyptus, and slippery elm, which can be consumed as teas or added to soups and meals. By using these natural remedies, individuals can alleviate symptoms and improve their overall quality of life, sleeping better, eating more comfortably, and enjoying everyday activities without the constant discomfort of a persistent cough.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its soothing properties, which calm the mucous membranes in the throat and reduce inflammation. The antioxidants present in licorice root also help to break down mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby relieving congestion and coughing spasms. Additionally, licorice has anti-inflammatory compounds that ease irritated airways, allowing for smoother breathing and less frequent coughing fits. Overall, herbal licorice decoctions provide natural relief from persistent and hacking coughs.
remedy_properties: Glycyrrhizin: This compound has anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which can help soothe a sore throat and reduce inflammation caused by respiratory infections that trigger coughing fits.
Liquiritin: This flavonoid has expectorant properties, meaning it helps loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughs.
Isomaltose: This sugar molecule has a soothing effect on the mucous membranes in the throat, providing relief from irritation and inflammation that can exacerbate coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins have anti-inflammatory properties, which help in reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and soothing coughs.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help in thinning mucus and making it easier to cough up, thereby providing relief from coughing fits.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help in reducing inflammation and oxidative stress in the respiratory tract, thereby providing relief from coughing fits and other respiratory issues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that has anti-inflammatory properties and can help soothe a sore throat.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and saponins, which can help reduce inflammation and calm coughing.
Stems: May be used to make decoctions that help to relax the muscles and calm the respiratory system, reducing coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 to 3 kilograms of fresh glycyrrhiza glabra roots in the early morning after the dew has evaporated.
Wash the roots in cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and weigh out 50 to 100 grams for decoction.
Combine the chopped roots with 1 liter of water in a large saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer for 10 to 15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced slightly.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herbal remedy contains compounds like quercetin and luteolin, which soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion that can trigger persistent coughs. Additionally, the decoction's mucilage content coats and protects the throat and airways, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm, providing relief from hacking coughs and chest congestion. This natural remedy has been used for centuries to alleviate respiratory issues and promote a healthy cough response.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Purple coneflower contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thus alleviating coughing fits caused by chronic bronchitis, asthma, or allergies.

Antimicrobial properties: The decoction's antimicrobial properties, particularly its ability to inhibit the growth of bacteria like Streptococcus pneumoniae and Haemophilus influenzae, help combat infections that can trigger persistent coughing fits.

Expectorant activity: Purple coneflower's expectorant properties, which are attributed to its saponin content, facilitate the clearance of mucus and phlegm from the lungs and airways, providing relief from congestion and hacking coughs.
remedy_constituents: Iridoids: Flavonoids (specifically, isoiridin and isoiridin glucoside): These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce the severity of coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Phenolic acids (specifically, caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid): These antioxidants help protect the respiratory system from oxidative stress, which can contribute to coughing fits by reducing inflammation and promoting healing.

Alkylphenol glycosides (specifically, echinacoside): This compound has anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory properties that help stimulate the body's natural defense mechanisms, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their high concentration of echinacosides and alkylamides, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate respiratory issues.
Flowers: They are used for their antiviral and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce the severity and duration of coughing fits.
Leaves: They are used due to their high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe the respiratory tract.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea flowers and roots in late summer or early fall when in full bloom.
Chop 1-2 teaspoons of dried echinacea purpurea roots and 2-3 teaspoons of dried flowers.
Combine the chopped herbs in a saucepan with 8 ounces of water.
Bring the mixture to a boil then reduce heat and simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction and discard the solids then drink the liquid hot or chilled.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which help loosen and clear out mucus from the lungs. The antibacterial compounds in thyme also combat infections that can cause persistent coughs. When brewed into a decoction, the volatile oils in thyme are released, providing instant relief by reducing inflammation and soothing the respiratory tract, effectively calming coughing spasms and providing a good night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Thyme decoctions help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up and relieve congestion.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Thyme contains compounds that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help alleviate symptoms of a cough and make breathing easier.
Antimicrobial properties: Thyme's antimicrobial activity helps combat infections such as bronchitis, pneumonia, and sinusitis, which are common causes of persistent coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Thymol: Thymol is a phenolic compound that helps to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits caused by infection or irritation.
Carvacrol: Carvacrol is a monoterpenic phenol that exhibits expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, thereby relieving coughing fits associated with congestion.
Cineole: Cineole is a terpene that has anti-inflammatory and bronchodilatory effects, helping to relax the airways and reduce coughing fits caused by asthma or other respiratory conditions.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antitussive (cough-suppressing) and expectorant properties to help relieve coughs and congestion.
Flowers: Employed for their soothing and calming effects, which can help to ease coughing fits and promote relaxation.
Roots: Utilized for their anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and alleviate coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves from a clean and healthy plant source in the morning.
Dry the freshly harvested thymus vulgaris leaves in a single layer at room temperature for 1-2 weeks.
Use a coffee grinder to finely crush 10-20 grams of dried thymus vulgaris leaves into powder.
Combine 2-3 grams of the powdered thymus vulgaris with 250 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the thymus vulgaris mixture for 10-15 minutes before straining the decoction through a cheesecloth into a separate container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Mullein decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its soothing properties that calm irritated mucous membranes in the throat and lungs. The plant's natural anti-inflammatory compounds reduce swelling and ease congestion, allowing for easier breathing and a reduction in coughing spasms. Additionally, mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out excess mucus, making it an effective natural remedy for relieving stubborn coughs and promoting a restful night's sleep.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Mullein's expectorant properties help loosen and remove mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion and coughing fits.

Anti-inflammatory effects: Mullein's anti-inflammatory compounds help reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits, and alleviate symptoms such as sore throats and hoarseness.

Soothing and calming effects: Mullein's mucilage content creates a soothing coating in the throat and airways, providing instant relief from coughing spasms and irritation, allowing for a restful night's sleep.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can contribute to coughing fits by providing relief from irritated airways.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Verbascum thapsus have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory system, thus reducing the occurrence of coughing fits.

Terpenoids (specifically, Verbascoside): Verbascoside is known for its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing fits caused by congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve coughs and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Stems: Utilized for their demulcent properties, which help soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the throat and airways, reducing coughing and irritation.
Roots: Employed for their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which aid in loosening and removing mucus from the respiratory tract, alleviating coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves from a trusted source for medicinal use.
Boil 500 milliliters of water in a saucepan for approximately 5-7 minutes to reach a rolling boil.
Combine the dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves with the boiling water for 10-15 minutes.
Reduce the heat to a simmer and allow the decoction to steep for an additional 20-30 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container and discard the solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The licorice-like compounds in fennel help to soothe and calm the throat and bronchial passages, reducing inflammation and irritation that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, fennel's antispasmodic effects help to relax the muscles in the respiratory tract, easing spasms and discomfort associated with persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Fennel's decoction helps to loosen and expel mucus from the airways, reducing congestion and relieving coughs by making it easier to clear out excess phlegm.

Anti-inflammatory property: Fennel's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce swelling in the respiratory tract, which can alleviate symptoms of coughing fits such as sore throats and irritated lungs.
Antispasmodic property: Fennel's decoction can help relax the muscles in the throat and airways, reducing spasms that can cause severe coughing fits and making it easier to breathe.
remedy_constituents: Foeniculin: Foeniculin, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help relieve coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and promoting the removal of mucus.
Anethole: Anethole, a phenolic terpene, has antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate coughing fits by relaxing the muscles in the airways and reducing inflammation.
Selinene: Selinene, a sesquiterpene, has anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties, which help relieve coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and suppressing the cough reflex.
remedy_parts: Seeds: They are used due to their carminative and expectorant properties, which help to relieve coughs and ease digestion.
Leaves: They are used due to their anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, which help to soothe the respiratory system and reduce coughing fits.
Roots: They are used due to their antispasmodic and expectorant properties, which help to calm the respiratory system and promote the removal of mucus.
remedy_recipe: Gather 2-3 teaspoons of dried foeniculum vulgare flowers and leaves from a reputable source.
Combine the foeniculum vulgare with 2 cups of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container.
Store the prepared foeniculum vulgare decoction in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yellow jessamine decoctions helps with coughing fits because it contains flavonoids, which have natural expectorant properties that help loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract. The decoction's anti-inflammatory compounds also soothe irritated airways, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughs. Additionally, its antitussive properties can help suppress coughing spasms, providing relief for those plagued by persistent and frequent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Expectorant properties: Herbal yellow jessamine decoctions help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to expel, thus relieving coughs.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The decoction has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce swelling and irritation in the respiratory tract, further soothing a sore throat and calming persistent coughs.
Antitussive properties: The decoction contains compounds that directly calm and suppress the cough reflex, providing relief from acute or chronic coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Gelsemine: Gelsemine, an alkaloid, helps with coughing fits by acting as an antitussive agent, reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing.
Gelseminine: Gelseminine, another alkaloid, contributes to the antitussive and expectorant properties of Gelsemium sempervirens, helping to relieve coughing fits and aid in the removal of mucus.
Glaucine: Glaucine, a quinazolinic alkaloid, has been shown to possess antitussive properties, helping to alleviate coughing fits by reducing the neural activity associated with coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used due to their high concentration of gelsemine, which has sedative and antispasmodic properties that help alleviate coughing fits.
Barks: The barks are used because of their ability to reduce inflammation and act as an expectorant, helping to relieve coughing fits.
Leaves: The leaves are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to calm the respiratory system and reduce coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2-4 ounces of dried gelsemium sempervirens root in the fall or early winter season.
Weigh and combine the dried root with 1 pint of boiling water in a saucepan.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-20 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the decoction in the refrigerator for up to 2 days before consuming it cold.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ma huang decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The herbs in the decoction, such as Ephedra sinica, work to thin and reduce congestion, allowing for easier expulsion of phlegm and other irritants. Additionally, ma huang's anti-inflammatory compounds help soothe irritated airways, reducing coughing spasms and discomfort. As a result, ma huang decoctions provide effective relief from persistent coughs and chest congestion.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Ma Huang helps to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, relieving congestion and coughing fits by increasing the production of phlegm and making it easier to expel.

Anti-inflammatory property: Ma Huang has anti-inflammatory compounds that reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which can help to soothe an irritated throat and chest, reducing coughing and discomfort.

Bronchodilatory property: Ma Huang's beta-phenethylamine (MA) alkaloids relax bronchial smooth muscle, widening airways and making it easier to breathe, which helps to alleviate wheezing, coughing, and shortness of breath.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine alkaloids: They help with coughing fits by acting as a bronchodilator, relaxing the muscles in the airways and increasing airflow to the lungs.
Isoeugenol: It helps with coughing fits by exhibiting expectorant properties, thinning mucus and making it easier to cough up.
Caffeic acid: It helps with coughing fits by showing anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling in the airways and alleviating irritation that can trigger coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Ephedra sinica are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their high concentration of ephedrine, which helps to relieve respiratory tract congestion.
Seeds: The seeds of Ephedra sinica are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Leaves: The leaves of Ephedra sinica are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their ability to warm the lungs and relieve coughs associated with colds and bronchitis.
remedy_recipe: Harvest ephedra sinica stems and leaves in the morning, when the plant is at its highest concentration.
Clean and dry ephedra sinica thoroughly, removing any impurities, and store in airtight containers for later use.
Combine 6-8 grams of ephedra sinica stems and leaves with 2 liters of water in a large pot.
Boil the mixture for 15-20 minutes, then reduce heat and simmer for another 30 minutes to extract active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a separate container, discard the solids and store the liquid in the refrigerator.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese peony decoctions helps with coughing fits because they possess potent anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The herbal combination works to soothe irritated respiratory tissues, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughing. Additionally, the natural expectorants in the decoction help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, providing relief from stubborn coughs and making it easier to breathe. As a result, Chinese peony decoctions offer a natural and effective way to alleviate coughing fits and promote respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Peony decoction helps loosen and clear phlegm from the lungs, reducing coughing fits by relieving congestion and promoting easy mucus elimination.
Anti-inflammatory property: Peony's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the airways and lungs, which helps alleviate coughing caused by respiratory infections or irritation.
Mucolytic property: Peony decoction breaks down thick mucus into smaller, more manageable particles, making it easier to expel and providing relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Isoquinoline alkaloids: These compounds help alleviate coughing fits by exerting an expectorant effect, facilitating the removal of mucus from the airways.

Flavonoids (specifically, quercetin and kaempferol): These phenolic compounds exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the respiratory tract, thereby alleviating coughing fits.

Saponins (specifically, paeoniflorin and albiflorin): These compounds have been shown to have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help regulate the immune response and reduce inflammation in the respiratory system, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: Paeonia lactiflora roots are commonly used to make decoctions for coughing fits because they contain paeonol, a compound with anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Barks: Paeonia lactiflora barks are used in decoctions to help alleviate coughing fits due to their mucilaginous properties, which help soothe the throat and reduce inflammation.

Flowers: Paeonia lactiflora flowers are used to make decoctions for coughing fits because they contain flavonoids and alkaloids, which have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that help relieve coughing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh roots of paeonia lactiflora in the spring or fall with a sharp knife or spade.
Wash the roots thoroughly under running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the roots into small pieces and combine with 10 parts water in a saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce the heat to a simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chinese date decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its unique combination of sweet and nourishing properties. The dates, also known as jujubes, are rich in vitamins A and C, which help to soothe and calm the mucous membranes, reducing inflammation and congestion in the chest and throat. Additionally, the herbs used in traditional Chinese medicine, such as licorice root and ginger, have natural expectorant properties that help to loosen and clear out mucus, providing rapid relief from persistent coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Soothes lung heat: The decoction helps to calm down excessive heat in the lungs, which can cause dry, hacking coughs and relieve discomfort.
Moisturizes the respiratory tract: The herbs in the decoction add moisture to the throat and airways, reducing inflammation and alleviating coughing fits caused by dryness or irritation.
Expels phlegm and wind: The decoction's properties help to eliminate excess mucus and phlegm from the lungs, as well as dispel cold wind (pathogenic factors) that may be contributing to the cough.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: Saponins in Ziziphus jujuba decoctions are believed to help with coughing fits by reducing inflammation and soothing the mucous membranes in the respiratory tract.

Phenolic acids: Phenolic acids, particularly gallic acid and caffeic acid, in Ziziphus jujuba decoctions have been found to exhibit anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help alleviate coughing fits by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the respiratory system.

Flavonoids: Flavonoids, such as quercetin and kaempferol, present in Ziziphus jujuba decoctions have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help with coughing fits by reducing inflammation, promoting mucociliary clearance, and protecting the respiratory tract from oxidative damage.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits, also known as jujubes, are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Roots: The roots are used to make decoctions for coughing fits due to their anti-inflammatory and antitussive properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 6-8 dried ziziphus jujuba fruits and 2 tablespoons of water for decoction.
Boil the water for 10-15 minutes to reach optimal temperature and pressure.
Combine the dried ziziphus jujuba fruits with 2 cups of boiling water and steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Store the herbal decoction in the refrigerator for up to 3 days before consumption.

The plant's leaves and flowers contain bitter compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the airways, thus alleviating congestion and discomfort associated with coughs. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory properties, which help reduce inflammation in the airways and alleviate coughing fits.

remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 to 30 taraxacum officinale roots and wash them thoroughly in cold water.
Chop the roots into small pieces using a sharp knife to increase surface area.
Combine the chopped roots with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Blueberry decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its unique combination of antiviral, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties. Expectorant properties: The mucilage present in blueberries helps to soothe and relax the airways, making it easier to expel mucus and phlegm from the lungs, providing relief from persistent coughs.

remedy_recipe: Gather 10-20 grams of dried vaccinium myrtillus leaves and flowers.
Boil one liter of water in a pot for ten minutes before adding the herbs.
Add the herbs to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer for fifteen minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Cool the decoction to room temperature within sixty minutes of preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Joe pye weed decoctions helps with coughing fits because its active compounds, including aristolochic acid and other alkaloids, have natural expectorant properties that loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways. The decoction's anti-inflammatory effects also help reduce swelling in the throat and bronchi, providing relief from congestion and irritation that can exacerbate coughing fits. remedy_properties: Expectorant: Joe Pye Weed decoctions have a natural ability to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.
Antitussive: The compounds present in Joe Pye Weed decoctions can help reduce the intensity of coughing fits by suppressing the cough reflex, providing relief from persistent coughing.

Seeds: The seeds of Joe Pye weed are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, which help to reduce inflammation in the throat and chest, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried eupatorium perfoliatum herb for every eight ounces of water.
Combine the dried herb and water in a medium saucepan and bring to a boil over high heat.
Reduce heat to low and simmer for 10-15 minutes or until the liquid has reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the decoction to cool and store it in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours before consumption.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural soothing properties, which calm irritated throats and airways. The apigenin in chamomile binds to beta receptors in the body, promoting relaxation and reducing inflammation that can exacerbate coughs. Additionally, chamomile's anti-inflammatory compounds help to reduce congestion and ease mucus production, allowing for easier breathing and a more effective clearance of phlegm from the lungs, providing relief from persistent and annoying coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Chamomile's anti-inflammatory properties help reduce swelling and irritation in the throat and bronchial passages, making it easier to breathe and alleviating coughing spasms.
Antispasmodic: Chamomile's antispasmodic properties relax the muscles in the airways and throat, reducing coughing fits by calming down the spasms that trigger them.

Expectorant: Chamomile's expectorant properties help loosen and clear out mucus and phlegm from the lungs and bronchial tubes, making it easier to expel them and relieve congestion, which can contribute to persistent coughing.
remedy_parts: Roots: Chamomile roots contain apigenin, an flavonoid that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making them effective in soothing and calming coughs.
Flowers: The flowers of the chamomile plant are rich in terpenoids and flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and relax respiratory muscles, helping to alleviate coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried matricaria chamomilla flowers measuring 2-3 teaspoons per serving.
Boil one quart of water in a pot for at least 10 minutes.
Add the measured dried matricaria chamomilla flowers to the boiling water and steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container.
Drink the warm decoction immediately, repeating every 2-3 hours as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Elder decoctions helps with coughing fits because they provide a soothing and calming effect on the respiratory tract. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory properties of elderflowers help to reduce congestion and ease the discomfort associated with coughs. Additionally, elderberry's mucilage coats the throat and lungs, providing relief from irritation and dryness. As the decoction is consumed, it helps to thin out mucus, making it easier to expel, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_properties: Antiviral properties: Elder decoction's antiviral compounds, such as flavonoids and terpenes, help combat viral infections like the common cold and flu, reducing cough frequency and severity.
Mucolytic properties: The saponins present in elder decoction help break down mucus, making it easier to expel from the lungs, providing relief from chest congestion and coughing fits.

Expectorant properties: Elder decoction's expectorant compounds, such as mucilage and flavonoids, stimulate the production of mucus and promote its clearance from the airways, helping to relieve coughs and congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties, making them effective in reducing congestion and relieving coughs.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes contain compounds that help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract and ease coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20 grams of dried sambucus nigra flowers and 10 grams of dried sambucus nigra leaves.
Combine the herbs in a large glass jar with 500 milliliters of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15 minutes then strain the liquid through a cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and store the decoction in an amber-colored glass bottle at room temperature for up to two weeks.
Take 20-30 milliliters three times daily as needed to alleviate coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Cattail decoctions helps with coughing fits because it contains a unique combination of antioxidants, anti-inflammatory compounds, and expectorants that work together to soothe and calm irritated airways. The mucilage extracted from cattails forms a protective film on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion, while its antimicrobial properties help combat infections that can exacerbate coughs. As a result, cattail decoctions provide quick relief from persistent coughing fits by loosening mucus, reducing irritation, and promoting easy expectoration.
remedy_properties: Mucilage content: Cattail contains a high amount of mucilage, a thick, protective substance that coats and soothes irritated throats, reducing inflammation and discomfort associated with coughs.
Antispasmodic properties: Cattail's decoction has been shown to possess antispasmodic properties, which help relax the muscles in the throat and chest, reducing spasms and coughing fits.
Expectorant activity: The saponins present in cattail can act as expectorants, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the cattail plant contain a high concentration of mucilage, which helps to soothe and calm irritated airways, providing relief from coughing fits.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes of the cattail plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the throat and chest, making it easier to breathe during coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Gather one tablespoon of dried phragmites australis herb per cup of boiling water.
Measure two cups of filtered water in a saucepan and bring it to a rolling boil.
Add the phragmites australis herb to the boiling water and reduce heat to simmer.
Allow the mixture to steep for 15-20 minutes or until reduced by half.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yanhusuo decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its unique ability to relax the respiratory tract and reduce inflammation. The active compounds in yanhusuo, such as corydaline and corydalis flavone, have natural analgesic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated airways and relieve congestion. As a result, decoctions made from yanhusuo can help alleviate coughing spasms and provide fast relief from stubborn coughs, allowing for smoother breathing and improved overall respiratory health.
remedy_properties: Expectorant property: Yanhusuo decoctions help to loosen and expel mucus from the lungs and airways, relieving congestion and coughing fits.
Anti-inflammatory property: The decoction's anti-inflammatory effects help reduce swelling in the throat and lungs, which can contribute to persistent coughs.
Antitussive property: Yanhusuo contains compounds that help suppress or relieve coughing fits by calming the cough reflex and soothing the mucous membranes.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Yanhusuo plant have been used to treat coughs and respiratory issues due to their high concentration of alkaloids, which help to reduce inflammation and relieve congestion.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the Yanhusuo plant contain a high amount of starch and mucilage, making them an effective remedy for soothing irritated throats and reducing coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Gather 6-8 grams of dried corydalis yanhusuo roots and clean them thoroughly with cold water.
Combine the cleaned roots with 4 cups of pure water in a ceramic or glass pot and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 30-40 minutes to allow active ingredients to infuse into the liquid.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean container discarding the solids.
Allow the strained decoction to cool down to room temperature before refrigerating it for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its unique properties that soothe and calm irritated throats. The mucilage extracted from marshmallow roots forms a protective barrier, coating the throat and chest lining to reduce inflammation and discomfort. This natural shield also lubricates the airways, easing congestion and making it easier to breathe, thus reducing the frequency and intensity of coughing fits. As a result, marshmallow decoctions provide fast and effective relief for individuals suffering from persistent coughs.
remedy_properties: Mucilage production: Marshmallow decoction contains mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and protects the mucous membranes in the throat and lungs, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by coughing fits.
Expectorant properties: The saponins present in marshmallow decoction help to break down and loosen mucus, making it easier to expel and providing relief from congestion and coughing.

remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in mucilage, a thick, protective substance that soothes and calms irritated throat tissues, providing relief from coughing fits.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes (underground stems) contain antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation and discomfort associated with coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Measure two tablespoons of dried marshmallow root and place it in a large glass jar.
Add one quart of boiling water to the jar, then cover and let steep for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another container.
Discard the solids and transfer the decoction to a saucepan, heating it over low heat until warm.
Pour the warm decoction into a mug and sip while hot to help soothe coughing fits.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural expectorant properties, which loosen and clear out mucus from the respiratory tract. The antibacterial and anti-inflammatory compounds in yarrow, such as achillein and sesquiterpenes, help to reduce inflammation and combat infections that can cause coughing. Expectorant Properties: Yarrow's decoction also has expectorant properties, which allows it to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier for the body to expel them, thereby relieving coughing fits.

remedy_recipe: Gather dried achillea millefolium flowers one ounce or five grams for each serving.
Measure one cup of water boiling point temperature add the herbs and steep.
Let the mixture simmer on low heat for 10 to 15 minutes releasing active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve discard solids retain liquid.
Store the cooled decoction in an airtight container refrigerate for up to three days.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Plantain decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and calming coughing spasms. The plant's mucilages also help to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion and hacking coughs. By loosening and clearing out phlegm, plantain decoctions ease breathing and alleviate discomfort, making them a natural remedy for managing chronic or acute coughs.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Plantain decoctions reduce inflammation in the throat, which helps alleviate coughing fits and soothes irritated mucous membranes.
Expectorant properties: Plantain's expectorant properties help loosen and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, making it easier to cough up phlegm and relieve congestion.

Antitussive properties: Plantain decoctions have a calming effect on the respiratory system, which helps reduce the frequency and intensity of coughing fits, providing relief from discomfort and pain.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in starch and mucilage, plantain roots help soothe irritated throats and reduce inflammation associated with persistent coughing fits.

Seeds: Plantain seeds contain fatty acids and antioxidants, which can help relax the respiratory tract and alleviate dry, hacking coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh plantago major leaves and stems for every cup of water.
Rinse the plant material thoroughly with cold running water to remove dirt and debris.
Combine the rinsed plantago major with 4 cups of filtered water in a large saucepan.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat then reduce to low heat for 10-15 minutes steeping time.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh strainer into a clean glass container.

supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to coughing fits that are treated with herbal decoctions are bronchitis, asthma, and pharyngitis. Decoctions made from herbs such as eucalyptus, ginger, licorice root, and slippery elm help to relieve respiratory congestion, reduce inflammation, and soothe irritated mucous membranes. These natural remedies can also help to calm coughs and ease breathing difficulties.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best combination of herbal decoctions that help with coughing fits is a blend of thyme, ginger, and licorice root. Thyme's antimicrobial properties can help combat underlying infections, while ginger's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe the throat and reduce irritation. Licorice root's expectorant properties can help loosen and clear mucus, making it easier to cough up. Combine 1 teaspoon of dried thyme with 1 teaspoon of dried ginger and 1 teaspoon of dried licorice root in 8 ounces of boiling water, then strain and drink 2-3 times a day to help alleviate coughing fits.
intro_study: For example, according to a study published by Alternative therapies in health and medicine, herbal decoctions are beneficial for coughing fits due to the results found in the study, where patients in the Chinese medicinal herbs (CMH) groups showed a higher Traditional Chinese Medicine (TCM) recovery rate, better relief of primary symptoms including cough and postnasal dripping, reduction in physical signs, and a lower risk of cough relapse compared to those in the control groups. The study, which reviewed 16 studies published in Chinese journals, found that CMH may be a safe and effective alternative for the treatment of Upper Airway Cough Syndrome (UACS). The meta-analysis conducted using a random effects model, due to the poor homogeneity of the studies, supported the effectiveness of CMH in alleviating cough symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain active compounds that stimulate the immune system and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract. The plant's phenolic acids and alkylamides have been shown to have antiviral and antibacterial properties, which can help combat underlying infections that trigger coughing. Additionally, Echinacea's anti-inflammatory properties can soothe irritated mucous membranes, making it easier to breathe and reducing the severity of coughing fits. This can provide relief and comfort to individuals experiencing persistent or chronic coughing.
remedy_constituents: Caffeic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, which contributes to coughing fits.
Alkylphenols: They possess immunomodulatory effects that enhance the body's immune response, which helps in fighting off infections that cause coughing fits.
Iridoid glycosides: They exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties that help reduce inflammation and promote the clearance of mucus from the airways, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: They are used due to their rich content of phenolic compounds and alkylamides, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and immune-modulating properties.
Leaves: They are used because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that may help to soothe and calm the respiratory system.
Flowers: They are used for their high content of alkylamides and phenolic compounds, which are thought to have expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve coughs.
remedy_recipe: Harvest echinacea purpurea roots and flowers from a clean and organic source during the summer months.
Dry the harvested echinacea roots and flowers in a low-temperature oven or a dehydrator.
Chop the dried echinacea roots and flowers into smaller pieces to increase their surface area.
Combine one tablespoon of chopped echinacea roots with one quart of boiling water to create a decoction.
Steep the echinacea mixture for 10 to 20 minutes before straining and serving.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain compounds that have anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The licorice root extracts in these decoctions can soothe and calm the mucous membranes in the throat, reducing inflammation and irritation that often triggers coughing fits. Additionally, the expectorant properties help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, relieving congestion and making it easier to breathe. Liquiritin: This flavonoid glycoside has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, making it easier to cough up.
Licoric acid: This phenolic compound has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, which help to soothe and calm the airways, reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are the most commonly used part due to their high glycyrrhizin content, which has anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties.
Leaves: The leaves are used to make decoctions that help in soothing coughs and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Barks: The barks are used in decoctions to treat coughs and colds due to their antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties.
remedy_recipe: Gather 3-5 grams of dried roots and stems of the medicinal plant and clean them thoroughly.
Boil 1 liter of water in a saucepan and then add the prepared plant material.
Reduce heat to a simmer and let the mixture steep for 10-20 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and then refrigerate or store it in a dark container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Thymus vulgaris decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its expectorant properties that aid in loosening and clearing mucus from the lungs and airways. The thymol present in the herb has antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which help reduce congestion and irritation in the respiratory tract. Additionally, Thymus vulgaris decoctions contain compounds that help soothe the throat and calm coughing spasms, providing relief from persistent and irritating coughs. Caryophyllene: Caryophyllene exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing swelling and irritation in the throat and lungs, thereby alleviating coughing fits.
Rosmarinic acid: Rosmarinic acid has antimicrobial properties, fighting off infections that cause coughing fits, and its anti-inflammatory properties help in reducing throat irritation.

Buds: Thyme buds are sometimes used to make decoctions, as they contain a higher concentration of thymol and other active compounds that help to relieve coughs and congestion.
remedy_recipe: Gather 1 tablespoon of fresh or 2 teaspoons of dried thymus vulgaris leaves for decoction preparation.
Chop the gathered leaves into small pieces to increase their surface area for infusion.
Combine the chopped leaves with 8 ounces of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce heat and let the mixture simmer for 5 to 10 minutes to release the active ingredients.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a cup to remove the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain compounds that exhibit anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. remedy_recipe: Gather 1 teaspoon of dried zingiber officinale root and 2 cups of water for decoction.
Boil the water for 5 minutes and add the dried root to the boiling water.
Reduce heat to low and simmer the mixture for 5-7 minutes or until the liquid reduces.
Strain the decoction through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Discard the solids and serve the decoction warm or at room temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eucalyptus globulus decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its decongestant and anti-inflammatory properties. The main compound, eucalyptol, works to thin mucus and make it easier to expel, providing relief from congestion and coughs. Additionally, eucalyptus globulus has expectorant properties, helping to break up and clear mucus from the lungs and airways, thereby reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits. remedy_constituents: Eucalyptol: It helps with coughing fits by reducing inflammation in the airways and acting as a bronchodilator, making it easier to breathe.

Alpha-pinene: It helps with coughing fits by acting as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, and also by reducing inflammation in the airways.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are used due to their high concentration of eucalyptol, a compound that helps to ease congestion and relieve coughs.

Stems: The stems are used due to their high content of eucalyptol and other volatile oils, which can help to break down mucus and ease coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Measure 1 teaspoon of dried eucalyptus globulus leaves and add it to a small pot.
Pour 1 cup of boiling water over the dried leaves and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup.
Discard the solids and let the decoction cool to room temperature or slightly warmer.
Drink the decoction immediately and repeat as needed up to 3 times a day.

remedies.remedy_desc: Verbascum thapsus decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain mucilages, a type of soluble fiber that forms a soothing gel-like substance when mixed with water. This gel-like substance coats the throat and airways, reducing inflammation and irritation, which can be caused by coughing fits. As a result, Verbascum thapsus decoctions can provide quick relief from coughing fits by calming the throat and airways, making it easier to breathe and swallow. remedy_constituents: Iridoid glycosides: These compounds help to reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract, which in turn reduces coughing fits.
Phenolic acids: By exhibiting antimicrobial properties, phenolic acids help to combat infections that often cause coughing, thereby alleviating the condition.

Triterpenoids: Triterpenoids have been found to possess anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe the mucous membranes in the respiratory system, thus reducing the frequency and severity of coughing fits.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of Verbascum thapsus are used to make decoctions due to their high mucilage content, which helps to soothe and calm the respiratory tract.
Leaves: The leaves of Verbascum thapsus are used to make decoctions to help relieve coughs and colds due to their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs.

Flowers: The flowers of Verbascum thapsus are used to make decoctions to treat coughs and colds due to their demulcent and antitussive properties, which help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, and relieve coughing fits.
remedy_recipe: Collect 2 to 4 grams of dried verbascum thapsus flowers and leaves in late summer or early fall.
Crush the collected dried plant material into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle.
Combine the crushed powder with 200 milliliters of boiling water in a heat-resistant container.
Steep the mixture for 10 to 15 minutes allowing the flavors and properties to infuse.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine mesh into a clean container discarding the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Mentha x piperita decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its natural antitussive and anti-inflammatory properties. The essential oils present in peppermint, such as menthol and menthone, have a soothing effect on the respiratory tract, reducing irritation and inflammation that can exacerbate coughing. The decoction also helps to thin mucus, making it easier to expel, and can calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety-related coughing. Its expectorant properties further aid in loosening and removing mucus, providing relief from coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Menthol: It helps with coughing fits by providing a cooling sensation, which can temporarily numb the throat and reduce irritation.
Rosmarinic acid: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate inflammation in the throat and airways, reducing coughing.
Menthone: It has expectorant properties, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the respiratory tract, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of Mentha x piperita are commonly used to make decoctions for coughing fits because they contain menthol, which helps to ease congestion and reduce coughing.
Roots: The roots of Mentha x piperita are used to make decoctions for coughing fits because they contain menthone, a compound that helps to relax the muscles in the throat and reduce coughing.
Stems: The stems of Mentha x piperita are used to make decoctions for coughing fits because they contain menthol and other compounds that help to ease congestion and soothe the throat.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh mentha x piperita leaves and flowers from your garden or a trusted supplier.
Clean and chop the harvested mentha x piperita leaves and flowers into small pieces for better extraction.
Combine the chopped mentha x piperita with 200ml of boiling water in a heat-resistant glass or ceramic container.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes to allow the active compounds to infuse into the water.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass container to discard the solids.

remedies.remedy_desc: Lavandula angustifolia decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its soothing and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughing. The decoction's antimicrobial properties also help combat underlying infections that may be contributing to the cough. By relaxing the airways and reducing inflammation, Lavandula angustifolia decoctions can provide relief from persistent and distressing coughing fits.
remedy_constituents: Linalool: It acts as an expectorant, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the airways, reducing coughing fits.
Linalyl acetate: It has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce inflammation in the throat and airways, alleviating coughing.
Camphor: It has a numbing effect on the throat and airways, providing temporary relief from coughing by reducing the sensitivity of nerve endings.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Lavandula angustifolia leaves are used due to their calming and soothing properties that help alleviate coughs and congestion.
Flowers: Lavandula angustifolia flowers are used for their antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which help to calm coughing fits and reduce inflammation in the respiratory tract.
Stems: Lavandula angustifolia stems are used for their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of fresh lavandula angustifolia flowers or 10-15 grams of dried flowers.
Dry the harvested flowers in a single layer on a paper bag for 7-10 days.
Combine 1-2 teaspoons of dried flowers with 1 quart of boiling water and let steep for 5-10 minutes.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Allow the decoction to cool and then refrigerate it for up to 3 days.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica decoctions helps with coughing fits because of its expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs. The decoction's active compounds, such as ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, also have anti-inflammatory effects that can reduce inflammation in the airways and improve breathing. Additionally, Ephedra sinica has a warming and drying effect that can help to reduce congestion and relieve coughing fits, making it a traditional remedy for respiratory issues. Its benefits are often used to combat conditions such as bronchitis and asthma.
remedy_constituents: Ephedrine: Acts as a bronchodilator, helping to relax airway muscles and reduce coughing by increasing airflow and relieving congestion.
Sinapic acid: Exhibits anti-inflammatory properties, reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract and alleviating coughing fits associated with conditions like bronchitis and asthma.
N-methyltyramine: Displays expectorant and bronchodilatory effects, helping to loosen and clear mucus from the lungs, making it easier to cough up and relieving congestion.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: Rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of ephedrine and pseudoephedrine, which help reduce coughing.
Barks: Barks are used due to their expectorant properties, which help relieve coughing by loosening and expelling mucus.
Seeds: Seeds are used due to their diuretic properties, which help relieve coughing by reducing inflammation and fluid buildup in the body.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 30-60 grams of ephedra sinica roots and cut them into small pieces to release their medicinal properties.
Dry the ephedra sinica pieces in a low-temperature oven at 50-60°c for 2-3 hours to preserve their potency.
Combine the dried ephedra sinica pieces with 6-8 cups of water in a large pot to create a decoction base.
Bring the mixture to a boil and then reduce the heat to a simmer for 1-2 hours to extract the active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to remove the solids and obtain the liquid extract.

remedies.remedy_desc: Sambucus nigra decoctions helps with coughing fits because they contain bioactive compounds, particularly flavonoids and phenolic acids, which exhibit potent anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. These compounds help to soothe and calm the respiratory tract, reducing inflammation and congestion that can exacerbate coughing fits. remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds have anti-inflammatory and antiviral properties, which help reduce the severity and frequency of coughing fits by soothing the respiratory tract and combating underlying infections.

Phenolic acids: These compounds exhibit antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory activities, which help alleviate coughing fits by inhibiting the growth of pathogens and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.

Triterpenoids: These compounds possess expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help relieve coughing fits by thinning mucus, promoting its clearance from the airways, and reducing inflammation in the respiratory tract.
remedy_parts: Flowers: They are used due to their high content of antiviral and antimicrobial compounds that help soothe and calm coughs.
Leaves: They are used because of their expectorant properties, which help to loosen and clear mucus from the airways.
Fruits: They are used due to their high content of vitamin C and other compounds that help to boost the immune system and fight off underlying infections causing the cough.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 1-2 cups of fresh or dried sambucus nigra flowers or berries from a trusted source.
Clean the harvested sambucus nigra thoroughly to remove any dirt or debris from the plants.
Combine 1 cup of sambucus nigra with 2 cups of water in a saucepan and bring to a boil.
Reduce the heat and simmer the sambucus nigra decoction for 10-15 minutes to release its active compounds.
Strain the decoction through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container to discard solids.
